observation: I can see I'm on a country/residence selection page. The page shows 'Select your residence' heading and there's a country dropdown with 'Select' placeholder. I can see many country options in the list including Indonesia (183), India (181), Brazil (101), Australia (71), and many others. There's also a 'Next' button (439) at the bottom. I need to select a country first before proceeding. From my previous actions, I see I tried Indonesia and Japan already. Looking at the visible countries, I should pick one that's likely to be accepted by this financial platform.
reasoning: As a 70-year-old user, I need to select my country of residence to continue the sign-up process. I can see many countries available in the dropdown list. Since this appears to be a financial/trading platform (Deriv), and I've already tried Indonesia and Japan in previous steps, I should select a different country that's commonly accepted. Australia is a good choice as it's a major market and visible in the list at element 71.
ux_issues: The country list is very long and requires scrolling to find options
Small text size may be difficult for elderly users to read
No search functionality visible to quickly find countries

dual_diagnosis.visual_observation: The dropdown menu is not visible in the AFTER screenshot. The Country/region field still shows 'Select' placeholder text and no country appears to be selected. The interface remains in the same state as BEFORE.
dual_diagnosis.diagnosis: The tap action on Australia option did not register or the dropdown menu was not properly opened to show the country options. The selection did not persist in the UI state.
